core.when: When implementing financial ratio calculations that require cross-statement data alignment
core.action: Implement period intersection logic to find common dates across income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow before calculating ratios; discard periods where any required statement is missing data

consequence.description: Without period intersection, ratios may combine Q1 income statement with Q2 balance sheet, producing nonsensical financial metrics; backtesting strategies on misaligned ratios creates systematic errors that don't reflect actual company performance

core.when: When handling data requests with time ranges
core.action: Verify request start timestamp does not exceed end timestamp before processing

consequence.description: Invalid time ranges would cause assertion errors or undefined behavior in data queries, crashing backtest runs

core.when: When preparing Fama-French factors and risk-free rate for OLS regression
core.action: divide percentage-formatted FF factors and risk-free rate by 100 before using in regression

consequence.description: FF factors stored as percentages (e.g., 5.2) must be converted to decimals (0.052); using raw percentages produces coefficients scaled by 100x and invalid inference

core.when: When forecasting models use past observed covariates
core.action: Verify past covariate lags are negative integers (before current time) — past covariates represent historical data available at prediction time

consequence.description: Using non-negative or invalid past covariate lags causes look-ahead bias where the model uses future information not available at prediction time, invalidating forecast accuracy metrics

core.when: When computing forward returns from price data
core.action: use shift(-period) to correctly align future prices with current factor values

consequence.description: Using shift(period) instead of shift(-period) introduces look-ahead bias, causing future information to be used in historical analysis and producing backtests that cannot be replicated in live trading

evidence_refs.summary: src/alphalens/utils.py:312 - forward_returns = returns.shift(-period).reindex(factor_dateindex)

core.when: When training ML models on historical financial data
core.action: Use point-in-time (PIT) data that reflects only information available at each historical date — adjusted prices and fundamental data must be PIT-corrected

consequence.description: Training on forward-filled or revised data creates look-ahead bias, causing models to fail in live trading when using only available information

evidence_refs.summary: 01_machine_learning_for_trading/README.md:133 'carefully manage and curate data to avoid look-ahead bias by adjusting it to the desired frequency on a point-in-time basis'

core.when: When calculating max drawdown in performance analysis
core.action: Use cumulative maximum minus current value pattern to identify peak-to-trough decline

consequence.description: Max drawdown calculation will fail to identify the true peak-to-trough decline, leading to incorrect risk assessment and Calmar ratio

core.when: When incrementing stock day data in MongoDB
core.action: query the last stored date from database and only fetch data after that date

consequence.description: Re-downloading existing data causes duplicate entries and incorrect backtest results due to overlapping timestamps

core.when: When implementing or refactoring thread-based strategy tracking in the follower module
core.action: Verify strategy tracking threads are configured as daemon threads using setDaemon(True) before starting — threads must not block program exit when main thread completes

consequence.description: Non-daemon threads prevent clean process exit, causing backtest scripts and automated trading systems to hang indefinitely after completion; this blocks subsequent scheduled runs and causes resource leaks

core.when: When comparing volatility estimates from indicators with risk metrics
core.action: Use consistent DDOF (degrees of freedom) across each statistical calculations - indicators must use ddof=1 if metrics use ddof=1, or explicitly document when DDOF differs between indicator calculations and risk metric calculations

consequence.description: Indicators use ddof=0 (population variance) while metrics use ddof=1 (sample variance); Bollinger Bands use population std while Sharpe Ratio uses sample std, causing band widths and risk estimates to be calculated differently, making strategies appear more or less risky than actual

core.when: When computing rolling volatility and Sharpe ratio
core.action: annualize volatility using sqrt(APPROX_BDAYS_PER_YEAR) where APPROX_BDAYS_PER_YEAR equals 252

consequence.description: Annualized volatility and Sharpe ratio will be incorrectly scaled if using calendar days instead of trading days

core.when: When computing technical indicators from price data
core.action: Apply shift(1) or equivalent lag to price-based indicators before using them as state features

consequence.description: Technical indicators computed on the current candle's close price create look-ahead bias — the agent sees information not yet available at decision time, inflating backtest returns and causing live underperformance

evidence_refs.summary: meta/factors/factors.py:80,192-204 apply .shift(N) to close_price before returning; meta/env_market_impact/envs/market_data.py:262 uses .shift(1).rolling() for volatility calculation
meta/factors/factors.py:80,192-204 apply .shift(N) to close_price before returning; meta/env_market_impact/envs/market_data.py:262 uses .shift(1).rolling() for volatility calculation

core.when: When implementing SMA or other TA-Lib functions to accept pandas Series input
core.action: Return pandas.Series with the same index preserved from the input Series

consequence.description: Breaking pandas index preservation causes downstream code to lose temporal alignment, leading to incorrect backtest results and misaligned signal generation

evidence_refs.summary: talib/__init__.py:67-69 extracts index from first pandas Series; lines 97-99 pass index to output Series constructor

core.when: When calibrating interest rate models (e.g., Hull-White) to swaption volatility surface
core.action: Use Levenberg-Marquardt optimization algorithm combining Gauss-Newton and gradient descent methods; verify initial parameters are within convergence radius

consequence.description: Incorrect optimization algorithm selection causes convergence failures or poor calibration quality, producing inaccurate risk factors that invalidate regulatory capital calculations

core.when: When passing price data to the portfolio simulator
core.action: Verify close price arrays have consistent datetime index and float64 dtype across each symbols

consequence.description: Portfolio simulation may produce incorrect trade records and position valuations when price arrays have mismatched indexes, causing financial calculation errors in PnL and returns

core.when: When calculating equal-weighted portfolio returns in performance analysis
core.action: Use median for cross-sectional demeaning in equal-weighted portfolio calculations instead of mean — this provides robustness to outlier constituent returns

consequence.description: Using mean for demeaning makes equal-weight returns sensitive to extreme constituent returns, causing backtest results to diverge from live trading where outlier positions have disproportionate impact on portfolio performance

evidence_refs.summary: Derived from BD: BD-046

core.when: When instantiating the data catalog
core.action: Enforce singleton pattern to prevent multiple catalog instances pointing to different data sources

consequence.description: Multiple catalog instances could cause inconsistent data queries, missing or duplicate data, and non-deterministic backtest results

core.when: When passing internal pandas.Series from _run() computation to wrapper method accessors
core.action: return Series that preserve the original close price index for temporal alignment

consequence.description: If returned indicator Series lose the original time index, downstream feature aggregation will misalign indicators with each other and with price data, causing incorrect backtest results

evidence_refs.summary: All indicator accessor methods (e.g., ta/momentum.py:53-60, ta/trend.py:122-131) create Series using index=self._close.index to preserve temporal alignment
All indicator accessor methods (e.g., ta/momentum.py:53-60, ta/trend.py:122-131) create Series using index=self._close.index to preserve temporal alignment

core.when: When implementing or modifying MRR computation logic in evaluation code
core.action: Use average rank for ties when computing MRR — do not implement random tie-breaking or single-rank assignment for duplicate prediction scores

consequence.description: Modifying tie-breaking logic introduces non-determinism into evaluation metrics, making backtest results non-reproducible and preventing fair model comparison across experiments

core.when: When implementing volatility targeting with rolling window periods
core.action: account for NaN values in the first N periods before the rolling window completes

consequence.description: Volatility-adjusted leverage will be NaN for the initial periods equal to the rolling window (vol_periods), causing backtest P&L series to contain NaN values for warmup periods and potentially causing downstream calculation errors in portfolio aggregation

evidence_refs.summary: src/finmarketpy/backtest/backtestengine.py:2875-2876 explicitly sets lev_df[0:vol_periods] = np.nan to ignore elements before the vol window kicks in; rolling_volatility at line 2848 requires vol_peri

core.when: When preparing datasets for machine learning model training and validation
core.action: Split data temporally into TRAIN, VALID, TEST segments using date range boundaries — verify each TRAIN timestamps precede VALID, which precedes TEST

consequence.description: Random or incorrect temporal splitting introduces look-ahead bias where future information leaks into training data, causing backtest results to significantly overestimate live trading performance

core.when: When implementing regression analysis in the timeseries module
core.action: Use statsmodels OLS for regression calculations to preserve full statistical inference capabilities (t-stats, R-squared, confidence intervals)

consequence.description: Replacing statsmodels OLS with numpy lstsq or sklearn LinearRegression strips statistical inference capabilities, making backtest results unreliable for hypothesis testing and confidence estimation

core.when: When implementing Double Exponential Moving Average (DEMA) indicator
core.action: Calculate DEMA using the formula: DEMA = 2*EMA(period) - EMA(EMA(period), period). This specific coefficient structure achieves approximately half the lag of standard EMA. Do not simplify or re-derive the formula.

consequence.description: Incorrect DEMA formula variations produce different lag characteristics that alter signal timing. Short-term strategies relying on DEMA crossovers will generate signals at different prices, causing backtest results to diverge from expected short-term trading behavior.

core.when: When calibrating equity implied volatility surfaces for arbitrage-free interpolation
core.action: Use SVI (Stochastic Volatility Inspired) parameterization with 5 parameters per maturity — do not use raw spline interpolation as it risks calendar spread arbitrage and produces unstable surfaces

consequence.description: Raw spline interpolation of volatility surfaces can produce arbitrage opportunities, causing Monte Carlo and tree models to price derivatives inconsistently and generate exploitable arbitrage profits in backtesting

core.when: When calculating forward prices across different option pricing models
core.action: Use S*exp(r*t) for Black-Scholes forward and S*exp((r-q)*t) for Black-Scholes-Merton forward, where q is the continuous dividend yield

consequence.description: Forward price mismatch will cause incorrect option pricing — the dividend adjustment in BSM forward formula is essential for accuracy with dividend-paying stocks

evidence_refs.summary: py_vollib/black_scholes/__init__.py:81 computes 'F = S / deflater' where deflater = exp(-r*t); py_vollib/black_scholes_merton/__init__.py:86 computes 'F = S * numpy.exp((r-q)*t)' with dividend adjustm

py_vollib/black_scholes/__init__.py:81 computes 'F = S / deflater' where deflater = exp(-r*t); py_vollib/black_scholes_merton/__init__.py:86 computes 'F = S * numpy.exp((r-q)*t)' with dividend adjustm

core.when: When parsing datetime strings from CSV
core.action: use the exact dtformat parameter matching the source file format

consequence.description: Datetime parsing will raise ValueError at runtime when the format string doesn't match the actual date format in the CSV file, causing data loading to fail silently or crash the entire backtest

core.when: When processing fund data or calculating positions at historical dates
core.action: Assume the framework provides point-in-time data - the framework lacks historical snapshot capability and uses current data for historical calculations

consequence.description: Without point-in-time data, historical backtests use current fund information for past dates, causing look-ahead bias where fund mergers, splits, or fee changes are incorrectly applied retroactively

core.when: When computing survival rates from cumulative PD
core.action: Verify sR[k] = 1.0 - cPD[k] for each periods k to verify probability conservation

consequence.description: Violating the constraint sR + cPD = 1 means probability mass is not conserved. At period T with certainty of outcome (default or survival), the probabilities must sum to exactly 1.

evidence_refs.summary: Survival rate calculation at model.py:672 computes 'sR[k] = 1.0 - cPD[k]'. The acceptance hint requires 'sum of survival and cumulative PD = 1 at each period'

core.when: When implementing triple barrier labeling logic
core.action: Verify barrier touch time t1 is always greater than or equal to the event timestamp

consequence.description: If t1 is less than the event timestamp, the triple barrier labels will exhibit temporal causality violation, causing the ML model to learn from future information and produce misleading backtest results that cannot be replicated in live trading

evidence_refs.summary: mlfinlab/labeling/labeling.py:60-93(get_events) - The get_events function computes t1 (barrier touch time) from t_events, and the docstring confirms t1 is event's endtime, which logically must be >= s

core.when: When using position purge functionality
core.action: preserve event ordering when reapplying fills for state reconstruction

consequence.description: Incorrect event ordering during position rebuild produces wrong realized PnL, quantity, and average price calculations, corrupting position state.

core.when: When implementing total return index construction with multiplicative cumulation
core.action: Initialize TRI at base value 100 and compound returns forward using cumprod

consequence.description: Additive cumulation (cumsum) allows negative cumulative returns to break the index chain, causing TRI values to become meaningless for P&L calculation and potentially producing misleading backtest results

evidence_refs.summary: src/finmarketpy/curve/fxspotcurve.py:40-43 and src/finmarketpy/curve/fxforwardscurve.py:351-354 show multiplicative formula out[i] = out[i-1] * (1 + return) with base 100; 'mult' is default at src/fin

src/finmarketpy/curve/fxspotcurve.py:40-43 and src/finmarketpy/curve/fxforwardscurve.py:351-354 show multiplicative formula out[i] = out[i-1] * (1 + return) with base 100; 'mult' is default at src/fin

src/finmarketpy/curve/fxspotcurve.py:40-43 and src/finmarketpy/curve/fxforwardscurve.py:351-354 show multiplicative formula out[i] = out[i-1] * (1 + return) with base 100; 'mult' is default at src/fin

core.when: When implementing or validating a transition matrix in the system
core.action: Verify each row sums equal 1.0 within the specified accuracy tolerance (default 1e-3)

consequence.description: Invalid transition matrices produce incorrect probability calculations, leading to flawed credit risk assessments and regulatory non-compliance

evidence_refs.summary: transitionMatrix/model.py:220-221: rowsum validation check against 1.0 with accuracy threshold

core.when: When validating position state during order execution
core.action: validate position is finite during each order processing

consequence.description: Infinite or NaN positions cause arithmetic failures in size calculations and corrupt trade PnL records

core.when: When simulating entry signals in backtesting
core.action: Process entry signals at current candle open price, not future candles

consequence.description: Entry signals using future price data create look-ahead bias, causing backtest results to be unrealistically profitable

core.when: When loading currency-converted equity pricing data
core.action: use shift=1 to align FX rates with prior-day prices

consequence.description: Incorrect shift causes look-ahead bias where future FX rates are used, producing misleading portfolio valuations that don't reflect actual trading conditions

core.when: When implementing position management logic in backtesting or live trading
core.action: Use exactly 1 for long positions, 0 for neutral, and -1 for short positions — the Position class requires discrete integer values for correct position state determination throughout the risk control and trading execution pipeline

consequence.description: Using non-discrete values (e.g., floats, enums, or other encodings) causes position state determination to fail silently, leading to incorrect risk calculations and potentially catastrophic position mismatches in live trading

core.when: When writing daily bar data to bcolz format
core.action: verify each asset has exactly one row per trading session with no gaps or duplicates

consequence.description: The BcolzDailyBarWriter raises an AssertionError if the number of rows per asset does not match the expected session count. Missing or extra sessions cause incorrect data alignment during backtesting, leading to wrong price lookups.

core.when: When calculating the historical probability time series
core.action: Apply proper rolling window calculation using only data available up to each historical date point

consequence.description: Using future data in historical calculations introduces look-ahead bias, causing historical probabilities to appear more accurate than they actually were and invalidating backtested performance metrics

core.when: When processing order records to build trade records
core.action: validate order size is greater than zero before creating trade records

consequence.description: Zero or negative order sizes in trade generation produce invalid trade records with corrupted size fields and incorrect PnL calculations

core.when: When validating order IDs in trade generation
core.action: verify order IDs come in ascending order per column during trade pairing

consequence.description: Non-ascending order IDs cause trade pairing to fail, producing incorrect entry/exit matches and corrupted PnL calculations

core.when: When implementing data ingestion to data filtering data flow
core.action: Maintain chronological order with no temporal gaps in OHLCV bar timestamps

consequence.description: Backtest equity curve exhibits look-ahead bias when non-chronological data causes indicator values to reference future prices, leading to live trading returns that fall far below backtested results

core.when: When processing price data inputs for portfolio optimization or backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework automatically detects or validates data freshness — the framework does not implement stale data detection or cache TTL policies

consequence.description: Without stale data detection, outdated prices are used silently causing portfolio weights to be based on stale market data, leading to execution failures and tracking errors in live trading

core.when: When implementing symbol ownership mapping for equity assets
core.action: create overlapping ownership periods for the same symbol in the same country

consequence.description: Overlapping symbol ownership periods cause ambiguous asset resolution, leading to incorrect security selection during backtesting and potentially trading the wrong asset

core.when: When implementing order direction logic in backtesting
core.action: Use positive order amounts for buy (long) positions and negative amounts for sell (short) positions — maintain consistent sign convention throughout order processing and position calculation

consequence.description: Inverting the order sign convention causes buy orders to be interpreted as sells and vice versa, resulting in completely opposite trading behavior with catastrophic losses in backtest and live trading

core.when: When computing technical analysis signals
core.action: Use shift(-n) to access future data in signal computation paths

consequence.description: shift(-n) in signal computation introduces look-ahead bias, causing backtest equity curve to show unrealistic returns that will not match live trading

core.when: When implementing time range generation for gap-filling
core.action: Request adjacent intervals to fill gaps rather than individual timestamps to reduce request overhead

consequence.description: Inefficient gap-filling would cause excessive data requests, longer backtest initialization times, and potential API rate limit issues

core.when: When implementing indicator-based signal generation
core.action: introduce inherent 1-period lag through rolling window or shift operations

consequence.description: Signals generated without lag will exhibit look-ahead bias, causing live trading returns to fall far below backtested results because the strategy would have traded on information not yet available

evidence_refs.summary: src/finmarketpy/economics/techindicator.py:92-93(rolling mean inherently produces lagged values) and tests/test_techindicator.py:55(expected_df = data_df.shift()) shows indicator aligns with shifted p

src/finmarketpy/economics/techindicator.py:92-93(rolling mean inherently produces lagged values) and tests/test_techindicator.py:55(expected_df = data_df.shift()) shows indicator aligns with shifted p

core.when: When implementing rolling performance metrics
core.action: align window to current timestep without including future data using i+1-window slicing

consequence.description: Rolling window must use past data only (current index excluded) to avoid look-ahead bias; using current or future data in rolling calculation inflates metric accuracy and produces misleading live trading signals

core.when: When preparing historical data for backtesting
core.action: Verify startup candle count equals or exceeds longest indicator lookback period

consequence.description: Insufficient startup candles cause indicators to reference future data, introducing look-ahead bias that inflates backtest performance

core.when: When extracting round trips from transactions DataFrame
core.action: Verify transactions DataFrame contains required columns: amount, price, symbol

consequence.description: Missing required columns causes KeyError exceptions during round trip extraction, breaking PnL calculation and duration computation

core.when: When fetching market data from external data sources
core.action: Implement fallback mechanism when data fetch returns None

consequence.description: Network failures, API errors, or missing credentials cause fetch_market to return None; without fallback, the entire backtest fails without generating any results

evidence_refs.summary: finmarketpy_examples/tradingmodelfxtrend_example.py:139-144 shows explicit None check with CSV fallback

core.when: When calculating long and short allocations
core.action: Define net liquidation as sum of longs plus shorts plus cash when computing allocation percentages

consequence.description: Net liquidation denominator excluding cash would misrepresent the true portfolio size, causing long/short allocations to not sum to 100% and leading to incorrect leverage calculations

core.when: When implementing standard deviation calculations for risk metrics
core.action: Verify ddof=1 is consistently used in each standard deviation calculations for sample variance estimation, regardless of whether bottleneck or numpy path is used

consequence.description: Inconsistent ddof values between bottleneck and numpy paths cause silent accuracy divergence where risk metrics differ between environments, violating correctness guarantees for backtest reproducibility

evidence_refs.summary: efe6066 Derived from BD: BD-094

core.when: When implementing or refactoring price collection and timestamp handling
core.action: Assume price data timestamps are unambiguous; the framework does not clearly distinguish between as-of date and processing timestamp

consequence.description: Without clear as-of vs processing time distinction, prices may be attributed to incorrect trading days, causing incorrect historical valuations and P&L calculations

core.when: When calculating position cost basis and profit, the FIFO (First In First Out) matching rule MUST be used
core.action: Apply FIFO matching for each position calculations to verify consistent cost basis across the system

consequence.description: Portfolio PnL calculations will be inconsistent with regulatory reporting if non-FIFO matching is used, causing compliance issues in live trading

core.when: When calculating security allocation amounts
core.action: Divide percent value by 100 before multiplying with portfolio total value

consequence.description: Allocation amounts will be 100x larger than intended, causing over-investment in securities and potential margin calls or regulatory violations

core.when: When purging closed positions from the cache
core.action: purge positions that are still open

consequence.description: Purging open positions causes position state to become inconsistent with actual trading state, leading to incorrect PnL and risk calculations.

core.when: When validating column positions before passing data to profit calculations
core.action: Implement explicit column position validation at the entry point of profit calculation logic, verifying that price columns (open, high, low, close) are in expected positions before processing

consequence.description: Without column position validation, corrupted profit figures silently propagate through CAGR, Sharpe, Sortino, MaxDrawdown, and other metrics, causing strategy selection based on backtest results that bear no relation to actual expected performance

core.when: When implementing indicators that depend on future data or require non-causal look-ahead
core.action: access positive array indices that represent future bars relative to current processing position

consequence.description: Indicator will exhibit look-ahead bias, producing unrealistic signals that cannot be executed in live trading based on historical data

core.when: When computing sample weights with time decay
core.action: produce negative weight values

consequence.description: Negative weights cause undefined behavior in sklearn classifiers, leading to invalid training outcomes or runtime exceptions when sample_weight is passed to fit methods

evidence_refs.summary: mlfinlab/sample_weights/attribution.py:59-62 documents decay parameter behavior: '0 < decay < 1 means that weights decay linearly over time, but every observation still receives a strictly positive we
